speaker: charlie
text: [Australian accent] Science and Tomorrow time - and scientists have achieved something they called 'the unthinkable.' They've stabilised a highly reactive molecule in water, confirming a decades-old theory about vitamin B1's role in our bodies.

speaker: alice
text: [Australian accent] Researchers in the UK are wrapping up trials on a revolutionary blood test that can detect around 50 types of cancer before symptoms even appear. Over 140,000 people took part, and if the results hold up, this could change everything about early detection.

speaker: charlie
text: [Australian accent] And quantum physicists at UC Santa Barbara have developed something called real-time Dyson expansion - basically a new way to understand how particles behave in driven quantum systems. Sounds fancy, but it's the kind of breakthrough that makes quantum computing actually work. Science nerds worldwide are probably having a cup of tea to celebrate.
